en:
Guide to Louvre Museum Visits

Planning your trip to France? Secure your Louvre Museum Paris tickets online and avoid the uncertainty. Our exclusive package provides you with valid Louvre entry tickets and a comprehensive Audio E-Guide to enhance your visit. Whether you are looking for Louvre tickets for a solo tour or a family visit, book now to guarantee your access to the world's most famous art museum.

Quick Facts

  • Ticket Requirement: Mandatory online booking for all visitors.
  • Best Time to Visit: Wednesday or Friday mornings.
  • Entrances: Pyramid (Main), Carrousel (Shopping Mall), Richelieu.

Types of Louvre Tickets Available

Choosing the right admission depends on how deep you want to dive into the history of art. Here are the most popular options available on our platform:

  • Standard Entry Ticket: Access to permanent collections and temporary exhibitions. Perfect for independent explorers.
  • Ticket + Audio Guide (E-Guide): Highly recommended. The museum is massive (60,600 square meters). The audio guide helps you navigate and understand the context behind masterpieces.
  • Combi-Tickets (Paris Saver): Combine your Louvre Museum entry with a Seine River Cruise or the Eiffel Tower to save money and time.

Why Buy Tickets Online?

Since the pandemic, the Louvre has introduced a strict time-slot system. Here is why you should never arrive without a ticket:

  • Skip the Ticket Line at the Cashier: Don't waste your holiday standing in queues. Digital tickets allow you to go straight to the security check.
  • Guaranteed Entry: Tickets for popular dates (especially weekends and holidays) sell out weeks in advance. Buying online secures your spot.
  • Instant Mobile Access: No printer? No problem. Show the QR code on your smartphone at the entrance.

How to Get to the Louvre?

The museum is located in the heart of Paris, on the Right Bank of the Seine (1st arrondissement).

  • By Metro: Take Line 1 or Line 7 and get off at Palais Royal - Musée du Louvre station.
  • By Bus: Lines 21, 24, 27, 39, 48, 68, 69, 72, 81, 95 all stop in front of the Pyramid.
  • By Batobus: Get off at the "Louvre" stop on the François Mitterrand quay.

Visitor Rules & Tips

Large bags and suitcases are not allowed inside. While photography is permitted, using flash or selfie sticks is prohibited near major artworks like the Mona Lisa to protect the paintings and ensure smooth visitor flow.
